title: Verb in URL — POST /createUser violates REST resource naming
expected_feedback: Critical: URL contains a verb ('createUser'). REST resources are nouns — the HTTP method IS the verb. POST /api/v1/createUser should be POST /api/v1/users. This breaks REST conventions, makes the API unpredictable (is it /getUser, /fetchUser, /retrieveUser?), and complicates OpenAPI tooling that expects resource-based paths. Additionally: response returns 200 instead of 201 Created, and is missing a Location header pointing to the created resource.
